title: helenium thurberi
intro: Helenium thurberi, also known as Thurber's sneezeweed, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Helenium thurberi can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is sometimes used topically to soothe skin conditions and promote wound healing.
